title: vSAN Component Overhead and Resync Backlog Depth Tracking
exclusions: Pure vSAN Express Storage Architecture clusters with materially different resync component accounting should carry a dedicated UC so field names and drain math stay accurate. Stretched or metro vSAN clusters need site-pair aware monitoring because bottleneck hosts may be meaningless without site context. Witness-only metadata repairs after witness reboots are often below paging thresholds by design. Single-node ROBO designs cannot enforce data fault tolerance the way multi-node clusters can, so backlog severities need different governance. This UC does not replace vendor Skyline emails or vSphere Lifecycle Manager gates.
evidence: Saved search uc_19_2_30_vsan_component_resync_backlog on index=vmware or index=hci with HEC sourcetypes above, alert history, and versioned vsan_resync_owner.csv for audit.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab, induce a non-destructive vSAN component resync, confirm vmware:vsan:resync:component shows non-zero bytes_to_resync within two one-minute polls, and verify the saved search returns the correct bottleneck host_id and a non-info severity when adaptive is off and backlog exceeds fifty gibibytes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Set change_freeze_until in vsan_resync_owner.csv to a future timestamp, reload the lookup, and confirm severity becomes info for rows that would otherwise be warning while backlog fields remain visible in the table for audit.

dataSources: PRIMARY (PowerCLI scheduled export to Splunk HEC): sourcetype=vmware:vsan:resync:component from Get-VsanResyncingComponent (Cluster, VM, Component, Bytes_To_Resync, per-component recovery rate or Recovery ETA seconds); sourcetype=vmware:vsan:host_runtime from Get-VsanRuntimeInfo per host (iops_resync, iops_capacity, latency_resync_us); sourcetype=vmware:vsan:cluster:adaptive from Get-VsanClusterConfiguration (AdaptiveResyncEnabled); sourcetype=vmware:vsan:host:maintenance from per-host maintenance context (EnsureAccessibility, EnsureFullDataMigration, NoAction or InMaintenance as your exporter encodes). SECONDARY: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380) sourcetype=vmware:perf and sourcetype=vmware:vc:event for datastore latency and resync or maintenance events. TERTIARY (optional): Splunk_TA_nix ingesting ESXi RVC output with sourcetype=vmware:rvc:vsan_resync. Governance lookup vsan_resync_owner.csv keyed on cluster_id.
app: PowerCLI scheduled export to HEC; Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder; (optional) Splunk_TA_nix for ESXi RVC
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.30 vSAN component resync queue depth, bottleneck host_id, cluster ETA, adaptive state; vmware|hci; vsan_resync_owner lookup; freeze demotes to info; earliest=-2h@h latest=@h")`
| search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resync:component" earliest=-2h@h latest=@h
| eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster, cluster_name, ClusterName, ""))
| eval host_id=toString(coalesce(host_id, hostId, host, hostname, esx_hostname, esx_host, ""))
| eval bytes_to_resync=tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_to_resync, BytesToResync, Bytes_To_Resync, "0")),10)
| eval bytes_per_second=tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_per_second, Bytes_Per_Second, bytesPerSecond, "0")),10)
| eval recovery_eta_sec=tonumber(toString(coalesce(Recovery_ETA_Sec, recovery_eta_sec, Bytes_Per_Second_Recovery_ETA_Sec, recovery_eta_seconds, "0")),10)
| eval bytes_per_second=if(((bytes_per_second<=0) OR isnull(bytes_per_second)) AND (recovery_eta_sec>0) AND (bytes_to_resync>0), bytes_to_resync/recovery_eta_sec, bytes_per_second)
| where (len(cluster_id)>0) AND (len(host_id)>0)
| stats sum(bytes_to_resync) AS host_bytes sum(eval(if((isnotnull(bytes_per_second)) AND (bytes_per_second>0), bytes_per_second, 0))) AS host_bps BY cluster_id host_id
| eventstats sum(host_bytes) AS cluster_bytes sum(host_bps) AS bytes_per_second_total BY cluster_id
| sort 0 cluster_id - host_bytes
| streamstats count AS rn BY cluster_id
| where rn=1
| eval active_resync_gib=round(cluster_bytes/pow(1024,3),3)
| eval eta_minutes=if((bytes_per_second_total>0), round((cluster_bytes/bytes_per_second_total)/60, 2), null())
| eval eta_minutes=if((isnotnull(eta_minutes)) AND (eta_minutes>525000), null(), eta_minutes)
| eval eta_display=if(isnotnull(eta_minutes), tostring(eta_minutes), "calculating")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:adaptive" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host:maintenance") earliest=-2h@h latest=@h | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster, cluster_name, "")) | eval _adaptive=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:adaptive", toString(coalesce(AdaptiveResyncEnabled, adaptive_resync_enabled, adaptive_state, "")), null()) | eval _maint=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host:maintenance", toString(coalesce(MaintenanceMode, maintenance_mode, VsanMaintenanceMode, vsan_maintenance_mode, EnsureFullDataMigrationMode, "")), null()) | eval _mhid=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host:maintenance", toString(coalesce(host_id, hostId, host, "")), null()) | eventstats latest(_adaptive) AS adaptive_resync_state BY cluster_id | eventstats values(_maint) AS maint_mode_list BY cluster_id | stats first(adaptive_resync_state) AS adaptive_resync_state first(maint_mode_list) AS maint_mode_list dc(_mhid) AS maint_host_count BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resync:component" earliest=-2h@h latest=@h | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster, cluster_name, ClusterName, "")) | eval bytes_to_resync=tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_to_resync, BytesToResync, Bytes_To_Resync, "0")),10) | bin span=10m _time | stats sum(bytes_to_resync) AS bin_bytes BY cluster_id _time | sort 0 cluster_id _time | streamstats window=2 current=f last(bin_bytes) AS prev_bin BY cluster_id | eval backlog_drain_trend_bytes=if(isnotnull(prev_bin), prev_bin-bin_bytes, 0) | stats latest(backlog_drain_trend_bytes) AS backlog_drain_trend_bytes latest(_time) AS trend_bucket_time BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [| inputlookup vsan_resync_owner | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id) | dedup cluster_id | fields cluster_id owner_team target_max_active_gib target_max_eta_minutes change_freeze_until ]
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(change_freeze_until)))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(cft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval targ_gib=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_max_active_gib, "999999")), 10)
| eval targ_eta=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_max_eta_minutes, "720")), 10)
| eval adaptive_on=if(match(lower(toString(adaptive_resync_state)), "(?i)true|1|enabled|yes|on"), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case((active_resync_gib<=0), "info", (isnotnull(eta_minutes)) AND (eta_minutes>=1440) AND (adaptive_on=0), "critical", (active_resync_gib>200) OR ((isnotnull(eta_minutes)) AND (eta_minutes>targ_eta) AND (active_resync_gib>targ_gib)), "high", (active_resync_gib>50), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval sev0=if((adaptive_on=1) AND (sev0="critical"), "warning", sev0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| eval adaptive_state=adaptive_resync_state
| table cluster_id host_id active_resync_gib eta_minutes eta_display adaptive_state severity owner_team change_freeze_until backlog_drain_trend_bytes maint_mode_list bytes_per_second_total freeze_active
description: Ingests component-level vSAN resync lines as vmware:vsan:resync:component, rolls bytes and instantaneous bytes-per-second to cluster and per-host views, picks the host_id holding the largest outstanding byte share as the bottleneck, joins adaptive resync and maintenance context, computes cluster ETA in minutes with a calculating fallback when rates are near zero, applies streamstats-based backlog drain trend samples in ten-minute bins, and routes severity using active backlog, ETA, governance targets, and adaptive pacing so operators can throttle foreground IO intelligently during disk replacement or evacuation.
value: Storage teams see calendar risk in real time: one slow ESXi node can stretch full fault-tolerance restoration across the whole cluster, and adaptive resync may be doing that on purpose. Quantifying backlog in gibibytes, naming the bottleneck host, and showing whether maintenance mode or adaptive policy explains the pace prevents duplicate incidents, misprioritised storage vMotion storms, and surprise pages when the UI already said the cluster was intentionally gentle on foreground workloads.
implementation: Install PowerCLI modules on a collector, deploy HEC pollers for the four vmware:vsan sourcetypes with 1 min active and 5 min idle cadence, add Splunkbase 5380 for vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event, optional RVC ingest via Splunk_TA_nix, upload vsan_resync_owner.csv, save the spl as uc_19_2_30_vsan_component_resync_backlog, schedule */5 * * * * with earliest=-2h@h.
visualization: Row 1 area chart active_resync_gib by cluster_id. Row 2 single value eta_display. Row 3 hosts table partial backlog. Row 4 adaptive_state versus maint_mode_list. Drills to vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event panels.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380)

knownFalsePositives: After adding fresh capacity to a disk group, vSAN may run a large proactive rebalance that resembles disaster recovery even though policy compliance is improving; cross-check Skyline rebalance triggers before a Sev-2. Skyline Health can initiate proactive resync checks that temporarily lift component counts without hardware loss. Adaptive resync during business hours legitimately throttles rebuild streams when congestion sensors fire, producing long ETA values that should not drive the same response as hardware faults. A witness metadata appliance reboot can leave a small, short-lived witness component repair that should stay below your witness noise floor. A storage policy downgrade or RAID geometry edit can legitimately burst resync traffic until the new layout stabilises; pair Splunk rows with the matching change record.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

This use case answers a narrow VMware vSAN operations question: at component granularity, how deep is the resync queue in bytes, which ESXi host currently anchors the largest share of that backlog, what is the cluster-level instantaneous drain rate implied by summed bytes per second, and is adaptive resync or maintenance mode explaining intentional slowness. It is deliberately not UC-19.2.9, which stays vendor-agnostic across HCI stacks and rebuild language that fits Nutanix and HyperFlex as well as vSAN. It is not UC-19.2.17 disk-group mount health, not UC-19.2.23 witness quorum, not UC-19.2.27 cluster slack and usable reserve percent, and not UC-19.2.37 VxRail-specific FTT evacuation headroom. When any of those light up, open the sibling saved searches in parallel; the component queue remains the authoritative per-host bottleneck view for vSAN resync math after disk replacement, host reboot, controller update, or partial evacuation windows.

Index strategy: converge feeds on index=vmware or a unified index=hci so PowerCLI pollers and the Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder share one retention class and one RBAC role for platform teams. If you duplicate to a second index during migration, deduplicate in production dashboards until cut-over finishes.

Primary sourcetypes from customer-operated HEC pollers: vmware:vsan:resync:component carries one JSON object per component per poll from Get-VsanResyncingComponent with stable keys cluster_id and host_id plus Bytes_To_Resync or camelCase mirrors, optional Bytes_Per_Second, Recovery ETA seconds, VM name, component identifier, and poll_utc. vmware:vsan:host_runtime carries Get-VsanRuntimeInfo fields iops_resync, iops_capacity, latency_resync_us on the same dimensional keys so operators can pivot from Splunk to vSphere without re-labeling hosts. vmware:vsan:cluster:adaptive carries AdaptiveResyncEnabled from Get-VsanClusterConfiguration. vmware:vsan:host:maintenance carries maintenance mode intent strings such as EnsureAccessibility, EnsureFullDataMigration, NoAction from maintenance enumeration consistent with your PowerCLI version.

Secondary Splunkbase path: Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder provides vmware:perf for latency and IOPS context during drain windows, and vmware:vc:event for resync-related and maintenance-related events that anchor incident timelines. These feeds do not replace the component poller because vSAN Health REST does not expose the same per-component repair queue for all builds; PowerCLI remains primary.

Tertiary optional path: Splunk_TA_nix on a hardened jumphost can ingest ESXi RVC snapshots such as vsan.resync_dashboard and vsan.proactive_rebalance_info with sourcetype=vmware:rvc:vsan_resync when API throttling blocks aggressive PowerCLI cadence. Treat RVC as corroboration and war-room evidence rather than the numeric contract for alerting.

Lookup file lookups/vsan_resync_owner.csv must exist on Search Heads with transforms.conf stanza [vsan_resync_owner], filename=vsan_resync_owner.csv, min_matches=0, case_sensitive_match=false. Header columns required: cluster_id, owner_team, target_max_active_gib, target_max_eta_minutes, change_freeze_until. cluster_id must match the same normalisation you emit from PowerCLI, whether MOID, display name, or URN string; never mix two styles in one deployment without a translation table. owner_team routes tickets and pages. target_max_active_gib and target_max_eta_minutes provide governance ceilings so a warm cluster with many small rotating jobs does not page the same as a single large disk rebuild. change_freeze_until holds an ISO-8601 or date-only timestamp that Splunk parses to freeze_active and demotes routed severities to info while still logging the row for audit.

Service accounts: vCenter least-privilege with vSAN read and storage views, not interactive admin. ESXi diagnostic or local shell access for RVC is separate from Splunk automation and should follow jump-host standards. PowerCLI execution identity on Windows or PowerShell Core on Linux must use credential objects bound from a vault or OS keychain, never plaintext in Task Scheduler arguments.

HEC design: dedicated token hci_vsan_resync, TLS, acknowledgement recommended, indexes restricted to vmware or hci. Polling cadence switches automatically: one minute while any component shows non-zero Bytes_To_Resync for a cluster, five minutes after two consecutive idle polls for that cluster. Document the state machine in the automation repository so capacity reviewers understand median vCenter session count.

PowerCLI prerequisites: Install-Module VMware.PowerCLI; Import-Module VMware.VimAutomation.Storage before long loops using Get-VsanResyncingComponent. Use Connect-VIServer with certificate validation against enterprise CA. Wrap Disconnect-VIServer in finally blocks. For sessions that exceed one hour during large rebuilds, reconnect inside the loop every N clusters as VMware documents for your release to avoid silent cmdlet failure mid-export.

Hardware lenses: vSAN ReadyNode and OEM equivalents such as Dell PowerEdge R650 R750 R760, HPE ProLiant DL380 DL360 Gen10 Gen11, Lenovo ThinkSystem SR650 SR635, Cisco UCS C-series rack models, Inspur and Fujitsu PRIMERGY lines, supporting all-flash disk groups, hybrid cache and capacity tiers, vSAN Express Storage Architecture clusters and Original Storage Architecture clusters as purchased, with 25 gigabit Ethernet or 100 gigabit Ethernet storage networks so adaptive resync has headroom and Splunk charts are not confounded by an artificial NIC ceiling.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

Implement a scheduled task on a management host that runs PowerShell, enumerates vSAN-enabled clusters permitted in governance, and posts JSON lines to Splunk HEC. The outer wrapper first calls a lightweight probe cluster list; if any cluster shows active resync, set next invocation delay to sixty seconds; else three hundred seconds. Persist last state in a small file under ProgramData or var lib to avoid flapping.

Illustrative PowerShell excerpt only; add logging, TLS, retry on 429, and secret retrieval in production. Note that maintenance mode cmdlets vary by PowerCLI major; substitute the command sequence your VMware support note lists for the vCenter build.

```powershell
Import-Module VMware.VimAutomation.Core, VMware.VimAutomation.Storage
$base = @{ Authorization = "Splunk $env:VSAN_HEC_TOKEN" }
Connect-VIServer $env:VCENTER_FQDN -Credential (Get-VCenterCred) | Out-Null
foreach ($cl in Get-Cluster | Where-Object { $_.VsanEnabled }) {
  $cid = $cl.Id
  foreach ($row in Get-VsanResyncingComponent -Cluster $cl) {
    $etaSec = 0
    if ($null -ne $row.RecoveryETA) { $etaSec = [int][double]$row.RecoveryETA.TotalSeconds }
    $bps = 0.0
    if (($etaSec -gt 0) -and ($row.BytesToResync -gt 0)) { $bps = [double]$row.BytesToResync / [double]$etaSec }
    $e = [ordered]@{
      cluster_id = "$cid"
      host_id = $row.EntityRuntime.Host.Name
      vm = $row.VM.Name
      component = "$($row.Component)"
      bytes_to_resync = [int64]$row.BytesToResync
      bytes_per_second = $bps
      recovery_eta_sec = $etaSec
      poll_utc = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("o")
    }
    Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $env:VSAN_HEC_URL -Headers $base -Method Post -Body (ConvertTo-Json @{ event=$e; sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resync:component"; index="vmware" } -Depth 5) -ContentType "application/json"
  }
  foreach ($h in Get-VMHost -Cluster $cl) {
    $ri = $h | Get-VsanRuntimeInfo
    $he2 = [ordered]@{ cluster_id="$cid"; host_id=$h.Name; iops_resync=$ri.ResyncIops; iops_capacity=$ri.TrafficIops; latency_resync_us=$ri.ResyncLatencyUs; poll_utc=(Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("o") }
    Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $env:VSAN_HEC_URL -Headers $base -Method Post -Body (ConvertTo-Json @{ event=$he2; sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host_runtime"; index="vmware" } -Compress) -ContentType "application/json"
  }
  $cfg = $cl | Get-VsanClusterConfiguration
  $adE = [ordered]@{ cluster_id="$cid"; AdaptiveResyncEnabled=[bool]$cfg.AdaptiveResyncEnabled; poll_utc=(Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("o") }
  Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $env:VSAN_HEC_URL -Headers $base -Method Post -Body (ConvertTo-Json @{ event=$adE; sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:adaptive"; index="vmware" } -Compress) -ContentType "application/json"
  foreach ($h2 in Get-VMHost -Cluster $cl) {
    $mm = (Get-View -Id $h2.ExtensionData.MoRef -Property Runtime.InMaintenanceMode).Runtime.InMaintenanceMode
    $mode = if ($mm) { "InMaintenance" } else { "NoAction" }
    $me = [ordered]@{ cluster_id="$cid"; host_id=$h2.Name; host_maintenance_mode=$mode; poll_utc=(Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString("o") }
    Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $env:VSAN_HEC_URL -Headers $base -Method Post -Body (ConvertTo-Json @{ event=$me; sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host:maintenance"; index="vmware" } -Compress) -ContentType "application/json"
  }
}
Disconnect-VIServer -Server * -Confirm:$false
```

Props and transforms: place the four vmware:vsan stanzas on indexers or heavy forwarders with JSON indexed extraction, SHOULD_LINEMERGE=false, TRUNCATE raised for wide component rows, TIME_PREFIX on poll_utc when present. Keep vmware:perf and vmware:vc:event on their vendor defaults from add-on 5380.

When PowerCLI exposes Get-VsanClusterMaintenanceMode on your vCenter build, replace the illustrative InMaintenance probe with the vendor enumeration EnsureAccessibility, EnsureFullDataMigration, or NoAction per object so maint_mode_list matches what operations sees in the vSphere client.

RVC tertiary: if you enable vmware:rvc:vsan_resync, scrub hostnames in dev exports and restrict index access because transcripts can include object paths.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Save the spl field verbatim as saved search uc_19_2_30_vsan_component_resync_backlog. Schedule cron */5 * * * * with time range earliest=-2h@h latest=@h. Alert trigger when severity is warning, high, or critical AND freeze_active is zero. Slack for warning on active_resync_gib above fifty gibibytes, page or ServiceNow P2 for high above two hundred gibibytes or lookup breach, P1 when ETA exceeds twenty-four hours unless adaptive pacing is intentional and documented in the ticket. Throttle per cluster_id for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates.

Canonical SPL for operator paste:

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.30 vSAN component resync queue depth, bottleneck host_id, cluster ETA, adaptive state; vmware|hci; vsan_resync_owner lookup; freeze demotes to info; earliest=-2h@h latest=@h")`
| search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resync:component" earliest=-2h@h latest=@h
| eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster, cluster_name, ClusterName, ""))
| eval host_id=toString(coalesce(host_id, hostId, host, hostname, esx_hostname, esx_host, ""))
| eval bytes_to_resync=tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_to_resync, BytesToResync, Bytes_To_Resync, "0")),10)
| eval bytes_per_second=tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_per_second, Bytes_Per_Second, bytesPerSecond, "0")),10)
| eval recovery_eta_sec=tonumber(toString(coalesce(Recovery_ETA_Sec, recovery_eta_sec, Bytes_Per_Second_Recovery_ETA_Sec, recovery_eta_seconds, "0")),10)
| eval bytes_per_second=if(((bytes_per_second<=0) OR isnull(bytes_per_second)) AND (recovery_eta_sec>0) AND (bytes_to_resync>0), bytes_to_resync/recovery_eta_sec, bytes_per_second)
| where (len(cluster_id)>0) AND (len(host_id)>0)
| stats sum(bytes_to_resync) AS host_bytes sum(eval(if((isnotnull(bytes_per_second)) AND (bytes_per_second>0), bytes_per_second, 0))) AS host_bps BY cluster_id host_id
| eventstats sum(host_bytes) AS cluster_bytes sum(host_bps) AS bytes_per_second_total BY cluster_id
| sort 0 cluster_id - host_bytes
| streamstats count AS rn BY cluster_id
| where rn=1
| eval active_resync_gib=round(cluster_bytes/pow(1024,3),3)
| eval eta_minutes=if((bytes_per_second_total>0), round((cluster_bytes/bytes_per_second_total)/60, 2), null())
| eval eta_minutes=if((isnotnull(eta_minutes)) AND (eta_minutes>525000), null(), eta_minutes)
| eval eta_display=if(isnotnull(eta_minutes), tostring(eta_minutes), "calculating")
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:adaptive" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host:maintenance") earliest=-2h@h latest=@h | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster, cluster_name, "")) | eval _adaptive=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster:adaptive", toString(coalesce(AdaptiveResyncEnabled, adaptive_resync_enabled, adaptive_state, "")), null()) | eval _maint=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host:maintenance", toString(coalesce(MaintenanceMode, maintenance_mode, VsanMaintenanceMode, vsan_maintenance_mode, EnsureFullDataMigrationMode, "")), null()) | eval _mhid=if(sourcetype="vmware:vsan:host:maintenance", toString(coalesce(host_id, hostId, host, "")), null()) | eventstats latest(_adaptive) AS adaptive_resync_state BY cluster_id | eventstats values(_maint) AS maint_mode_list BY cluster_id | stats first(adaptive_resync_state) AS adaptive_resync_state first(maint_mode_list) AS maint_mode_list dc(_mhid) AS maint_host_count BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [ search (index=vmware OR index=hci) sourcetype="vmware:vsan:resync:component" earliest=-2h@h latest=@h | eval cluster_id=toString(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, cluster, cluster_name, ClusterName, "")) | eval bytes_to_resync=tonumber(toString(coalesce(bytes_to_resync, BytesToResync, Bytes_To_Resync, "0")),10) | bin span=10m _time | stats sum(bytes_to_resync) AS bin_bytes BY cluster_id _time | sort 0 cluster_id _time | streamstats window=2 current=f last(bin_bytes) AS prev_bin BY cluster_id | eval backlog_drain_trend_bytes=if(isnotnull(prev_bin), prev_bin-bin_bytes, 0) | stats latest(backlog_drain_trend_bytes) AS backlog_drain_trend_bytes latest(_time) AS trend_bucket_time BY cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id [| inputlookup vsan_resync_owner | eval cluster_id=toString(cluster_id) | dedup cluster_id | fields cluster_id owner_team target_max_active_gib target_max_eta_minutes change_freeze_until ]
| eval cft=coalesce(strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), strptime(toString(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d"))
| eval freeze_active=if((isnotnull(change_freeze_until)) AND (len(trim(toString(change_freeze_until)))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(cft, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval targ_gib=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_max_active_gib, "999999")), 10)
| eval targ_eta=tonumber(toString(coalesce(target_max_eta_minutes, "720")), 10)
| eval adaptive_on=if(match(lower(toString(adaptive_resync_state)), "(?i)true|1|enabled|yes|on"), 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case((active_resync_gib<=0), "info", (isnotnull(eta_minutes)) AND (eta_minutes>=1440) AND (adaptive_on=0), "critical", (active_resync_gib>200) OR ((isnotnull(eta_minutes)) AND (eta_minutes>targ_eta) AND (active_resync_gib>targ_gib)), "high", (active_resync_gib>50), "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval sev0=if((adaptive_on=1) AND (sev0="critical"), "warning", sev0)
| eval severity=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev0)
| eval adaptive_state=adaptive_resync_state
| table cluster_id host_id active_resync_gib eta_minutes eta_display adaptive_state severity owner_team change_freeze_until backlog_drain_trend_bytes maint_mode_list bytes_per_second_total freeze_active
```

Step 3 — Validate

Navigate to vCenter, Cluster, Monitor, vSAN, Resyncing Objects, and read totals and per-host distribution for the same clock window as the Splunk poll. Open Skyline Health Operation Resync Objects if licensed; titles should correlate with non-zero ingested periods. For a lab-only proof, place one non-production host in maintenance with EnsureAccessibility, observe new component movement without full evacuation, capture Splunk output, exit maintenance. Never use EnsureFullDataMigration on production storage solely to test Splunk. When PowerCLI returns empty components while the UI lists items, verify VMware.VimAutomation.Storage import and build compatibility. When RVC output disagrees on ordering, use RVC for screenshots in the change record and PowerCLI JSON as the automation source of truth.

This validation narrative is written for component backlog and host bottleneck identification; it does not duplicate UC-19.2.9 generic rebuild totals, UC-19.2.17 disk group checksum focus, UC-19.2.27 usable-used slack, UC-19.2.37 VxRail FTT gate, or UC-19.2.23 witness heartbeat.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard layout: area chart of active_resync_gib by cluster_id across the picker; single value panel for eta_display with clear calculating state; table of hosts with partial sums for war rooms; adaptive_state single value; optional overlay of vmware:perf datastore latency for context. Runbook: during high or critical severities, pause discretionary storage vMotion, defer large snapshot consolidations, and ask application owners to reduce IO churn until drain improves. Escalate vendor support when ETA above twenty-four hours with adaptive off and healthy hardware. Ownership stays with owner_team from lookup and the platform bridge documented in the CMDB.

Relate to UC-19.2.9 for cross-stack rebuild storytelling, UC-19.2.37 for Dell-aware maintenance gating, UC-19.2.17 when disk-group faults might explain one slow node, UC-19.2.27 when slack is simultaneously tight.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

PowerCLI session drops mid-export: reconnect inside foreach and resume cluster list from a checkpoint file. Namespace missing for Get-VsanResyncingComponent: run Import-Module VMware.VimAutomation.Storage explicitly at task start. ETA shows calculating while UI shows hours: bytes_per_second_total below epsilon or stale; widen bin span or require two consecutive positive-rate samples. Adaptive resync on with low rate: treat as intentional pacing in paging policy, not a false clear. Maintenance with EnsureAccessibility: reduced protection is expected; annotate maint_mode_list before calling a Sev-1. Witness-only small resync burst after witness reboot: suppress pages below documented witness byte floor in governance addendum. Stretched clusters with asymmetric site links: host bottleneck may be site-local; pair with stretched-specific monitoring instead of this file alone. Certificate expiry on vCenter or HEC: renew and replay validation. Aggressive DRS storage motions during rebuild: correlate with vmware:vc:event to explain throughput dips that are not hardware faults.

Appendix A documents vsan_resync_owner.csv again for auditors: columns cluster_id, owner_team, target_max_active_gib, target_max_eta_minutes, change_freeze_until. Appendix B covers NTP alignment between vCenter, ESXi, poller host, and Splunk indexer to keep streamstats drain math honest. Appendix C states certificate rotation for vCenter and HEC twice yearly. Appendix D reminds that Splunk RBAC must include indexes vmware hci for roles that run the alert. Appendix E lists forbidden marketing language in internal wiki mirrors of this UC. Appendix F ties evidence packs to saved search history CSV exports with cluster_id and timestamp for sampled audits during PCI or internal storage reviews.


Appendix G expands collector sizing guidance for teams that run dozens of vSAN clusters from one automation host. Expect roughly one to three kilobytes per component per poll after JSON overhead; during a large disk-group replacement with thousands of components, a one-minute cadence can briefly push tens of megabytes per hour into the indexer, which is still modest relative to guest workload logs but must be budgeted in licensing reviews. When you auto-tighten cadence during active resync, also set a hard ceiling events-per-second on the poller so a pathology that explodes component counts cannot overwhelm vCenter sessions or HEC buffers.

Appendix H captures the cross-functional RACI pattern that makes this UC succeed in regulated enterprises. Storage operations owns the vsan_resync_owner.csv thresholds and the paging policy. Virtualization engineering owns PowerCLI versions and vCenter compatibility. Splunk platform engineers own HEC tokens, index retention, and search scheduling SLAs. Information security reviews the credential vault path quarterly. Lines of business sign off on target_max_eta_minutes when applications have batch cutovers that cannot tolerate long protection windows. Document those signatures in the change system, not only in email.

Appendix I restates onboarding tasks for new clusters. When a new vSAN cluster is built, add a cluster_id row to vsan_resync_owner before production traffic lands, validate poll visibility with a simple index=sourcetype search for fifteen minutes, attach the screenshot to the handover pack, and only then enable the alert. If you clone a build pattern from another data center, copy lookup rows carefully; a duplicated cluster_id string breaks dedup joins and sends pages to the wrong owner_team.

Appendix J addresses network partitions between the poller and vCenter. Symptom: no events while the UI still shows resync. First ping and traceroute to vCenter, then read Windows Event Log or systemd journal for TLS handshake errors, then confirm the service account is not locked. Splunk should show a gap on a home-rolled vmware:vsan:poller_health sourcetype if you add one; if you do not, at least alert on absence of vmware:vsan:resync:component events for known clusters using a second saved search.

Appendix K discusses coexisting with ITSI or SOAR. If you export severity and active_resync_gib to a KV store for ITSI thresholding, keep the KV update idempotent per cluster_id per minute so summary engines do not double-count. If SOAR opens a ticket, pass cluster_id, host_id bottleneck, adaptive_state, maint_mode_list, and a deep link to the vSphere Resyncing Objects view in your portal template.

Appendix L lists documentation debt checks after major vSphere upgrades. Re-run fieldsummary on each sourcetype, re-validate cmdlet output in a lab, update sample JSON fixtures in Git, and schedule a thirty-day review of false positives after the upgrade window closes.

Appendix M is for mixed OSA and ESA estates. If you operate both architectures, separate cluster_id prefixes in the lookup or add an architecture column you filter in a dashboard, because bytes-per-second distributions differ and governance targets may need per-architecture rows.

Appendix N warns against merging this alert with generic datastore fullness alerts. A datastore can be ninety percent utilized while component resync is idle, or fifty percent utilized while a massive repair is active; merging those ideas creates pager fatigue. Keep capacity siblings on UC-19.2.27 panels beside this queue panel.

Appendix O recommends backup and archive. Store twelve months online if your storage charter requires quarterly proof of proactive monitoring during rebuilds; move older summaries to frozen storage using Splunk bucket freezing policies aligned to your records team.
grandmaExplanation: When shared storage repairs itself after hardware work, copies can pile up on one machine and make the finish time hard to guess. We track those waiting bytes, name the busiest machine, see whether the system is slowing on purpose, and tell your team when it is safe to ease off other heavy storage tasks.
hardware: vSAN ReadyNode and OEM rack servers qualified for vSAN including Dell PowerEdge R650 R750 R760, HPE ProLiant DL380 and DL360 Gen10 and Gen11, Lenovo ThinkSystem SR650 SR635, Cisco UCS C-series rack nodes, Inspur platforms, Fujitsu PRIMERGY lines, with all-flash or hybrid disk groups per design, vSAN Express Storage Architecture and Original Storage Architecture deployments as purchased, and 25 or 100 gigabit Ethernet fabrics so resync and adaptive decongestion have realistic headroom.
